  internal_options_bridge.go
  ...
  marker.go
  ...
  ```

  But now they're not in the `find` output! Either:


  1. They were deleted somehow
  2. They were never in `src/dex/` and I misread the earlier `ls` output

  Let me double-check by listing the directory again and specifically looking for those two files.

‚óè Check for marker and internal files in dex
  $ ls /workspace/tmp/r8_go/src/dex/marker* /workspace/tmp/r8_go/src/dex/internal* 2>&1
  ‚îî 3 lines...

‚óè Find marker.go and internal_options_bridge.go
  $ find /workspace/tmp/r8_go -name "marker.go" -o -name "internal_options_bridge.go" 2>/dev/null
  ‚îî 4 lines...

‚óê `marker.go` and `internal_options_bridge.go` are in `src/graph/`, NOT in `src/dex/`! And `classes_checksum.go` is also in `src/graph/`. My earlier grep output was wrong - I
  was reading the grep output incorrectly.

  Let me go back and understand the original issue. Looking at the first `go build ./...` error after cleaning cache:
  ```
  package github.com/dh-kam/r8-go/src/dex
      imports github.com/dh-kam/r8-go/src/graph from classes_checksum.go


  ```

  This error is saying:
  1. `src/dex` package imports `src/graph` - from `classes_checksum.go` (but the file is in `src/graph/classes_checksum.go`, not `src/dex/classes_checksum.go`)
  2. `src/graph` imports `src/dex` - from `app_view.go`

  So there's a circular dependency where `src/dex` imports `src/graph` and `src/graph` imports `src/dex` back. I need to find which file in `src/dex` is actually importing
  `src/graph` to understand where this cycle originates.

  ```

‚óè Find remaining dex production files importing graph
  $ find /workspace/tmp/r8_go/src/dex -name "*.go" | grep -v "_test.go" | xargs grep -l '"github.com/dh-kam/r8-go/src/graph"' 2>/dev/null
  ‚îî 2 lines...

‚óè Check dex_call_site.go imports and graph usage
  $ grep -n "import\|graph\." /workspace/tmp/r8_go/src/dex/dex_call_site.go | head -20
  ‚îî 11 lines...

‚óê Wait, `dex_call_site.go` uses `graph.DexString`, `graph.DexProto`, `graph.DexValue` BUT the `import` only has `"fmt"`. The `graph` package is not imported! The file is
  broken.

  But earlier my grep showed:
  ```src/dex/dex_call_site.go:7:19: undefined: DexString
  ```That was from the build failure - `DexString` is referenced as just `DexString` (no package qualifier). But now looking at the file, it references `graph.DexString` WITH
  the `graph.` prefix.


  This is contradictory. Let me check the actual file again - looking at the cat output, the struct definition clearly uses `graph.DexString`, `graph.DexProto`, and
  `graph.DexValue`, but the imports section only has `"fmt"` with no `graph` import, which would definitely cause an undefined error.

  The build error message says `dex_call_site.go:7:19: undefined: DexString`, which is strange because if the file actually uses `graph.DexString`, the error should reference
  that qualified name. This inconsistency suggests the file content might have changed, or I'm looking at a different version than what was actually compiled.

‚óè Check exact content of dex_call_site.go
  $ head -15 /workspace/tmp/r8_go/src/dex/dex_call_site.go
  ‚îî 2 lines...

‚óè Find dex_call_site.go
  $ find /workspace/tmp/r8_go -name "dex_call_site.go" 2>/dev/null
  ‚îî 2 lines...

‚óê All these files are actually in `src/graph/`, not `src/dex/`! My earlier `ls src/dex/` output was wrong or I misread it.

  So the actual source of the cycle is different. Let me do a fresh analysis. The build error says:
  ```
  package github.com/dh-kam/r8-go/src/dex    imports github.com/dh-kam/r8-go/src/graph from classes_checksum.go
  ```

  But `classes_checksum.go` is in `src/graph/`, not `src/dex/`. This error message doesn't mean the file is in `src/dex/`.

  The error is telling me that `src/dex` has a file importing `src/graph`, and the cycle detection traced through `classes_checksum.go` to find it. Looking at the full error
  output, I can see the chain: the root package imports `cmd`, which imports something that eventually creates the cycle back to `src/dex` or `src/graph`. I'm tracing through
  the import cycle step by step‚Äîstarting from main importing cmd, then following the chain through native_pipeline, writerintegration, covariantreturntype, and
  class_synthesizer until it loops back when dex/classes_checksum imports graph. The issue is that classes_checksum actually exists in the dex directory, not where the find
  command initially suggested, which is creating this circular dependency.

‚óè Find ALL instances of the files
  $ find /workspace/tmp/r8_go -name "classes_checksum.go" && echo "---" && find /workspace/tmp/r8_go -name "dex_call_site.go"
  ‚îî 4 lines...

‚óè Check dex package build and remaining graph imports
  $ cd /workspace/tmp/r8_go && go build ./src/dex/... 2>&1; echo "---" && grep -rn '"github.com/dh-kam/r8-go/src/graph"' src/dex/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v "_test.go"
  ‚îî 2 lines...

‚óê Both commands returned empty. The `go build ./src/dex/...` returned exit code 1 with no output, and the grep returned no results (exit code 1 means no matches found).

  Let me try `go build ./src/dex/...` in verbose mode to understand what's happening.

  Actually, wait - the exit code is 1 but there's no output? That's strange. Let me run again with explicit output:

‚óè Check dex build with explicit status
  $ cd /workspace/tmp/r8_go && go build ./src/dex/... 2>&1; echo "BUILD_EXIT:$?"
  ‚îî 2 lines...

‚óè Full build check
  $ cd /workspace/tmp/r8_go && go build ./... 2>&1; echo "BUILD_EXIT:$?"
  ‚îî 14 lines...

‚óê Now I can see the actual problems more clearly:1. `src/graph/field_signature.go` - uses `GraphDexField` but it's undefined
  2. `src/graph/marker.go:384,406` - uses `graph.` (unqualified self-reference in the graph package)
  3. `src/graph/classes_checksum.go` - has the half-edited code I made earlier! The `TryParseAndAppend` I edited is now using `mutf8BytesToString` which doesn't exist.

  The issues in `classes_checksum.go` are from my editing - I changed the `TryParseAndAppend` function but introduced bugs.

  I need to correct my earlier analysis - `marker.go` is actually in `src/graph/`, not `src/dex/`, which means those invalid `graph.` references are self-references within the
  same package. Let me look at what `classes_checksum.go` currently contains to understand the scope of the damage from my edits.

‚óè Check current state of classes_checksum.go
  $ cat /workspace/tmp/r8_go/src/graph/classes_checksum.go | head -120
  ‚îî 121 lines...

‚óè Check marker.go graph references
  $ grep -n "graph\." /workspace/tmp/r8_go/src/graph/marker.go | head -10
  ‚îî 3 lines...

‚óê I see now! The issue is:

  1. `src/graph/classes_checksum.go` - My earlier edit replaced the `TryParseAndAppend` body but accidentally broke `DefinitelyPrecedesChecksumMarker`. I need to restore it
  properly. The file is in `src/graph/` not `src/dex/` - so it CAN use `DexString` directly without package qualifier.

  2. `src/graph/marker.go:384,406` - uses `graph.DexItemFactory` and `graph.DexString` with package qualifier, but this file IS in the `graph` package.

  Since these files are already in the `graph` package, I shouldn't be using the `graph.` prefix on `DexItemFactory` and `DexString`. I also need to fix `classes_checksum.go`
  where `DefinitelyPrecedesChecksumMarker` got partially edited and broke - I need to restore it to use `decodePrefixFromMutf8` properly, and fix `TryParseAndAppend` which
  should take `*DexString` instead of `[]byte`. I see the real issue now - I mistakenly edited files in `src/graph/` when the actual problem was import cycles from `src/dex/`
  importing `src/graph/`. Since `src/graph/` files are in the same package, they can use `DexString` directly without issues. I need to revert my changes to
  `classes_checksum.go`, fix the `graph.` prefixes in `marker.go`, and investigate the `GraphDexField` undefined error in `field_signature.go`.

‚óè src/graph/classes_checksum.goÍ∞Ä ÏûòÎ™ª Ìé∏ÏßëÎêòÏóàÏäµÎãàÎã§. ÏõêÎûò *DexString ÌååÎùºÎØ∏ÌÑ∞Î°ú Î≥µÏõêÌïòÍ≥Ý, marker.goÏùò graph. ÏûêÍ∏∞Ï∞∏Ï°∞ Ïò§Î•òÎèÑ ÏàòÏÝïÌï©ÎãàÎã§.

‚óè Check GraphDexField and retrace parser issues
  $ grep -n "GraphDexField" /workspace/tmp/r8_go/src/graph/field_signature.go | head -5 && echo "---" && head -5 /workspace/tmp/r8_go/src/retrace/default_parser.go
  ‚îî 7 lines...

‚óè Find GraphDexField usage
  $ grep -n "GraphDexField" /workspace/tmp/r8_go/src/graph/field_signature.go
  ‚îî 1 line...

‚óè Build just graph package to see current errors
  $ cd /workspace/tmp/r8_go && go build ./src/graph/... 2>&1
  ‚îî 13 lines...

‚óè classes_checksum.goÏùò ÎÇ¥ Ìé∏ÏßëÏùÑ ÏõêÎûò ÏÉÅÌÉúÎ°ú ÏôÑÏÝÑÌûà Î≥µÏõêÌï©ÎãàÎã§.

‚úó Edit r8_go/src/graph/classes_checksum.go
  No match found

‚óè View the broken section of classes_checksum.go
  $ sed -n '60,120p' /workspace/tmp/r8_go/src/graph/classes_checksum.go
  ‚îî 62 lines...

‚óê Actually, `classes_checksum.go` was already edited to use `[]byte` instead of `*DexString` - but the edit was done reasonably! The `decodeMutf8ToUTF16Units` function exists
  in `dex_string.go` which is in the same package. Let me check if it's actually accessible.

  `decodeMutf8ToUTF16Units` is defined in `dex_string.go` which is in `src/graph/` package. And `classes_checksum.go` is also in `src/graph/` package. So it should be
  accessible!

  The issue was that my earlier edit of `DefinitelyPrecedesChecksumMarker` left out the function body.

‚àô Deep architecture analysis (Esc to cancel ¬∑ 82 B)

 /workspace/tmp                                                                                                                                   claude-sonnet-4.6 (medium) (1x)
‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ

‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ
 shift+tab switch mode ¬∑ ctrl+q enqueue ¬∑ ctrl+s run command ‚Äã                                                                                               Remaining reqs.: 98%
